pi_items.source: Verified via Columbia Law School Scholarship Archive (https://scholarship.law.columbia.edu/faculty_scholarship/156/), JSTOR (https://www.jstor.org/stable/i246871). Article is on constitutional fact review doctrine and appellate review standards. Injected into database from inject_references.py as a journal article with title 'Constitutional Fact Review', author Henry P. Monaghan, publication Columbia Law Review, volume 85, issue 2, pages 229-277, date 1985, ISSN 0010-1958.

Verified via multiple sources on Marbury v. Madison. The case's central holding concerns the distinction between original and appellate jurisdiction under Article III. The full note to be added is: 'Focus on Marshall's reasoning regarding original jurisdiction vs appellate jurisdiction.' The phrase 'original jurisdiction' is a key concept explicitly addressed in the opinion written by Chief Justice John Marshall.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a Jurism collection named 'Week 1 - Judicial Review' and adding two real legal references: (1) Marbury v. Madison, the landmark 1803 Supreme Court case (5 U.S. 137) by Chief Justice John Marshall establishing judicial review and addressing the distinction between original and appellate jurisdiction; and (2) 'Constitutional Fact Review,' a 1985 law review article by Henry P. Monaghan published in Columbia Law Review Vol. 85, Issue 2, pp. 229-277 (ISSN 0010-1958). A child note containing the phrase 'original jurisdiction' must be added to Marbury v. Madison, and an HTML report must be generated and saved to '/home/ga/Documents/week1_syllabus.html'. Both legal references are based on real, publicly documented sources.
